poster_title: Test-Time Preference Optimization: On-the-Fly Alignment via Iterative Textual Feedback
authors: Yafu Li, Xuyang Hu, Xiaoye Qu, Linjie Li, Yu Cheng

content: Large language models (LLMs) show impressive capabilities.
LLMs trained on unlabeled text may produce unexpected responses.
Traditional alignment methods are time-consuming and less adaptive.
Align LLMs during inference to match performance of training-time methods.
Use interpretable textual feedback for preference optimization.
Ensure rapid and efficient adaptation to evolving data distributions.
Test-time Preference Optimization (TPO) aligns LLM outputs without parameter updates.
Policy model interprets reward signals and refines responses iteratively.
TPO operates as an online, on-policy learning paradigm.
Evaluated on benchmark datasets: AlpacaEval 2, Arena-Hard, HH-RLHF, BeaverTails-Evaluation, XSTest, MATH-500.
Tested on unaligned (Llama-3.1-70B-SFT) and aligned (Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct) models.
Compared TPO with traditional training-time methods.
TPO aligns policy model with human preferences, increasing reward model scores.
Unaligned Llama-3.1-70B-SFT surpasses aligned Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct on most benchmarks.
TPO scales efficiently with search width and depth during inference.
TPO achieves better alignment with human preferences than training-time methods.
TPO provides a lightweight alternative to traditional alignment methods.
TPO can be valuable in scenarios requiring rapid adaptation.
TPO is a practical and efficient method for inference-time alignment.
Leverages LLMs' ability to interpret and act on reward signals.
Suitable for scenarios where rapid adaptation is needed.
